news.summary: A prominent Democratic legislator initiated an investigation on Thursday into a private dinner hosted by President Donald Trump for high-profile investors in his Official Trump cryptocurrency. Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-Md.), who serves as the Ranking Member of the House Judiciary Committee, penned a letter to Trump requesting disclosure of the guest list for the gathering that took place last week. Raskin also questioned the source of the funds utilized by the investors to acquire the meme coin. Raskin stated, "Publication of this list will also inform the public about the individuals contributing tens of millions of dollars to the President, enabling scrutiny on what benefits they might be receiving in return aside from virtually worthless memecoins." The lawmaker also raised concerns about the attendance at the event. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Rep. Jamie Raskin seeks details on Trump's crypto dinner guest list, concerned about foreign investors buying access through meme coin.

news.summary: Investors who purchased BlackRock and Fidelity's spot Ether Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs) are currently experiencing an average unrealized loss of approximately 21%, according to data from Glassnode.
news.headline: Investors in Ethereum ETF facing significant losses. BlackRock and Fidelity's Ether ETF buyers at 21% loss: Glassnode.

news.summary: UP Fintech Holding Limited (NASDAQ: TIGR) is set to announce its first-quarter earnings before market opening on Friday, May 30. In the last quarter, the company reported a profit of 17 cents per share, surpassing the expected 11 cents per share. Its quarterly sales amounted to $124.10 million, higher than the projected $97.00 million. Despite this positive performance, UP Fintech shares experienced a 1.3% drop, closing at $8.43 on Thursday. For more detailed information and analyst ratings, refer to the Analyst Stock Ratings section on Benzinga.
news.headline: UP Fintech (TIGR) to report Q1 earnings on May 30. Q4 earnings: 17 cents/share, sales: $124.10M, beat estimates. Stock closed at $8.43.

news.summary: A recent report states that the global smartphone market witnessed a sharp decline in sales during the second quarter of this year. The total number of smartphone units sold dropped by 20% compared to the same period last year, reaching only 250 million units. This decline can be attributed to various factors, including supply chain disruptions due to the ongoing pandemic and decreased consumer demand. The leading smartphone manufacturers were also affected, with market leaders seeing a significant decrease in sales. Experts predict that the smartphone market will continue to face challenges in the upcoming months as the global economic situation remains uncertain.
news.headline: Oil faces second weekly decline before OPEC+ decision on supply; market on edge.

news.summary: Ulbricht emphasized the significance of unity within the cryptocurrency industry, stating that it is crucial to uphold their principles in the current climate.
news.headline: Ross Ulbricht addresses Bitcoin 2025, emphasizing the need for unity and adherence to principles in the crypto community.

news.summary: The upcoming 23rd China Kunming International Flower Expo (KIFE) and Kunming International Flowers & Plants Expo (IFEX) are set to occur from September 19 to 21, 2025, at the Kunming Dianchi International Convention and Exhibition Center in Kunming, China. This important event will showcase the latest trends and innovations in the flower and plant industry, attracting a significant number of participants and attendees from around the globe.
news.headline: Global flower industry thriving in Kunming at the 23rd KIFE and IFEX exhibitions from Sept 19-21, 2025 at Kunming Dianchi International Convention and Exhibition Center.

news.summary: Cryptocurrency market saw a decline on Thursday despite SEC dropping lawsuit against Binance. Bitcoin fell to $105,900.54 (-1.81%), Ethereum dropped to $2,626.22 (-2.71%), and Dogecoin to $0.2138 (-3.80%). Bitcoin's trading volume surged by 15%, leading to the overall market decline. However, Bitcoin was up 12.12% and Ethereum up nearly 45% for the month. In a separate development, the SEC ended its two-year lawsuit against Binance, accusing the exchange of securities law violations; Binance celebrated the win and credited SEC Chair Paul Atkins and ex-President Donald Trump.
news.headline: Bitcoin, Ethereum, Dogecoin prices drop; SEC ends Binance lawsuit; Bitcoin at $105,900.54; Ethereum $2,626.22; Dogecoin $0.2138.

news.summary: Funnull Technology has been accused by OFAC of acquiring a code repository utilized by web developers and modifying it to redirect authentic websites to fraudulent websites.
news.headline: US imposes sanctions on Filipino tech company for allegedly enabling crypto scams by altering code to redirect legitimate websites to fraudulent ones.

news.summary: The financial results of Canopy Growth Corporation (NASDAQ: CGC) for the fourth quarter will be announced on Friday, May 30. Analysts anticipate a loss of 20 cents per share for the Smiths Falls, Canada-based company. Benzinga Pro data suggests that Canopy Growth is expected to reveal quarterly revenue of around $71.83 million. In a recent development on April 1, the company disclosed the reinforcement of its balance sheet through an advanced prepayment of its senior secured term loan. Canopy Growth confirmed an early prepayment of $100 million from its senior secured term loan, leading to an estimated saving of about $13 million in interest expenses.
news.headline: Canopy Growth (NASDAQ: CGC) to report Q4 earnings; Estimated loss of 20 cents/share, revenue $71.83M. Paid $100M toward loan.

news.summary: In a recent development, it has been reported that the unemployment rate in the country has reached 6.1%, which marks a slight increase from the previous month. The number of job losses in the last quarter amounted to 450,000, with the majority of sectors experiencing a decline in employment opportunities. Additionally, the government has announced plans to introduce stimulus packages to address the economic challenges faced by the workforce. This news comes amidst growing concerns about the job market and financial stability of the population.
news.headline: Oil faces potential second weekly loss with OPEC+ set to make supply decision.

news.summary: Today, the stock market experienced a brief uptick, which was short-lived despite a special court declaring the majority of Trump's tariffs unlawful. Notably, Nvidia saw a notable increase. The market indexes showed signs of improvement following a meeting between Trump and Powell. Nvidia surpassed its entry point, while CoreWeave faced a significant downturn.
news.headline: Stock market indexes gain momentum post Trump-Powell meeting, with Nvidia surging past entry, but CoreWeave reverses.

news.summary: Israeli company TAT Technologies Ltd., listed on Nasdaq as TATT and TASE as TATT, disclosed on May 29, 2025, their pricing details for an underwritten offer. TAT is a prominent supplier in the commercial and military aerospace and ground defense sectors.
news.headline: TAT Technologies announces pricing of public offering of ordinary shares and secondary offering, targeting commercial and military industries.

news.summary: Trade talks between the United States and China have hit a standstill, with both countries waiting for input from their leaders to move forward, as reported recently. Discussions have slowed down, with U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stating that negotiations are currently stagnant. Bessent mentioned that a final agreement may necessitate direct involvement from President Donald Trump and China's President Xi Jinping. It has been two weeks since breakthrough talks overseen by Bessent led to a temporary halt in the trade dispute between the two biggest global economies. Despite this, progress has been slow, leaving both sides in a stalemate. For more information, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: US-China trade talks are stalling; Scott Bessent says Trump and Xi Jinping may be needed to break deadlock.
Stats show Industrials sector experienced a 10% growth, outperforming peers in the industry.

news.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced a significant drop of 800 points due to a rise in yields during Wednesday's trading session. Despite this decline, Nvidia saw a slight increase in its stock value, while Tesla also recorded a rise as anticipation builds for the launch of its robotaxi service scheduled for June. This news article highlights the impact of yield spikes and the pending vote on a bill related to former President Trump's policies on the stock market, signaling potential volatility and investor sentiment.
news.headline: Dow Jones drops 800 points due to rising yields; Tesla up before robotaxi launch. Trump bill to vote.

news.summary: A law firm, Robbins LLP, is currently investigating Proficient Auto Logistics, Inc. (NASDAQ: PAL) based in San Diego. The investigation aims to uncover any potential violations of securities laws and breaches of fiduciary duties by officers and directors of Proficient Auto Logistics. The company's stock is listed on the NASDAQ exchange under the symbol PAL. The investigation was announced on May 29, 2025.
news.headline: Robbins LLP is investigating Proficient Auto Logistics, Inc. (NASDAQ: PAL) officers and directors for possible securities law violations.

news.summary: Under the agreement, Astellas has obtained exclusive global rights (excluding China's mainland, Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan region) for the development and commercialization of XNW27011. This compound has shown promising efficacy as a monotherapy in an ongoing Phase 1/2 trial involving patients with solid tumors.
news.headline: Astellas gains exclusive global rights (excluding key regions) for XNW27011, a promising antibody-drug conjugate targeting CLDN18.2.

news.summary: The Trump administration recently dropped charges against Binance and its CEO, Changpeng Zhao, marking a step back in cryptocurrency enforcement. No specific numbers were mentioned in the original statement.
news.headline: S.E.C. drops lawsuit against Binance and founder, Zhao, as part of recent cryptocurrency enforcement changes.

news.summary: Realms of Alurya and Wonder Wars, two popular crypto games operating on the Ethereum network's Ronin platform, have recently joined a concerning, increasing trend of going offline. This trend highlights the challenges faced by such platforms, with a growing number of games being affected.
news.headline: Ethereum games Realms of Alurya and Wonder Wars shut down, joining the increasing trend of crypto games going offline.

news.summary: Stock market futures experienced an uptick following news of President Trump's decision to postpone imposing 50% tariffs on EU products. Investors are also keeping a close watch on Nvidia's upcoming earnings report. Additionally, Tesla is approaching a potential new buy point. This update indicates positive market movement with Dow Jones futures on the rise.
news.headline: Dow Jones Futures up after Trump delays EU tariffs; Nvidia earnings approaching, Tesla nearing new buy point.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was disclosed that the country's economy grew by approximately 3.5% in the last quarter, surpassing analysts' expectations. This marks a significant improvement from the previous quarter's growth rate of 2.1%. Additionally, the unemployment rate dropped to 4.5%, the lowest it has been in over a decade. The report also highlighted a 5% increase in consumer spending, indicating a boost in domestic demand. Experts attribute this positive economic performance to various factors, including increased industrial production and a rise in exports. The government has expressed optimism about the country's economic outlook for the coming months.
news.headline: Asian markets expected to open lower following fading US rally, stay updated as markets unfold.

news.summary: Shares of TXNM Energy, Inc. (NYSE: TXNM) are showing an increase in premarket trading as the company has accepted a buyout offer from Blackstone Infrastructure for $61.25 per share in cash upon completion. This acquisition sum signifies a 23.0% premium over TXNM Energy's previous 30-day volume weighted average price (VWAP) as of March 5, 2025, the day before rumors about a potential takeover surfaced. The deal values the company at $11.5 billion in total enterprise value, encompassing net debt (excluding securitization debt) and preferred stock. In addition, Blackstone Infrastructure will inject $400 million through a private placement agreement, acquiring 8 million shares.
news.headline: TXNM Energy stock surges after acquisition by Blackstone Infrastructure for $61.25/share in cash, 23% premium, total value $11.5B.

news.summary: Today, the stock market experienced a decline as President Trump announced potential tariffs on the European Union. This news caused stocks to fall. Additionally, an executive order from the White House led to a surge in nuclear stocks. Investors are also eagerly anticipating Nvidia's upcoming earnings report. This information was detailed in live coverage on Investor's Business Daily.
news.headline: Stocks fall after Trump threatens EU tariffs, White House boosts nuclear stocks; Nvidia earnings pending.

news.summary: U.S. stock market trading was mixed on Wednesday, with major indices showing limited movement as investors remained cautious due to concerns over fiscal policy. The S&P 500 was relatively unchanged near 5,930, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped by 0.7% driven by weakness in blue-chip stocks. In contrast, the Nasdaq 100 climbed by 0.5%, led by Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL) (GOOG) which rose by 4.6% following its annual I/O developer conference. Google announced significant AI initiatives, with Gemini assistant now having over 400 million monthly active users and providing AI Overviews to more than 1.5 billion individuals. The company also emphasized Gemini 2.5 Pro's strong performance in industry benchmarks. In bond markets, the 30-year Treasury yield surged to 5.02%, reflecting intensified selling pressure. For more details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Wall Street stalls amid rising yields, Bitcoin surpasses $109,000. Mixed trading sees S&P 500 at 5,930, Dow slips 0.7%, Nasdaq advances 0.5% led by Alphabet Inc.'s 4.6% rally. Treasury yield hits 5.02%.

news.summary: Midday on Friday, Wall Street experienced a decline due to heightened trade war concerns sparked by President Donald Trump's announcement of imposing new tariffs. Trump suggested a 25% import duty on Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL) if the company does not move its manufacturing operations back to the U.S. Additionally, he proposed a 50% tariff on European Union imports, raising worries about a potential transatlantic trade conflict. As a result, Apple Inc. (NYSE: AAPL) shares fell by 2.6%. The S&P 500 also saw a dip of 0.8%, falling below the significant 5,800 level and ending the week on a negative note. Sectors focused on growth, such as technology and consumer discretionary, were most affected by the selling pressure. For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Stocks tumble on Trump's tariff threats, Gold surges. Apple shares drop 2.6%, S&P 500 falls 0.8% below 5,800 mark.
OIL IS SET FOR ITS SECOND WEEKLY LOSS AS MARKETS AWAIT AN OPEC+ DECISION LIKELY TO BRING A THIRD CONSECUTIVE OUTPUT HIKE, RAISING CONCERNS ABOUT OVERSUPPLY.

news.summary: A recent report indicates that the global economy is projected to grow by 4.3% in 2021, following a 3.5% contraction in the previous year. This growth is largely due to a resurgence in economic activities as countries continue to recover from the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic. The report also mentions that emerging economies are expected to grow by 6.6% in 2021, while advanced economies are projected to grow by 3.9%. In terms of regional growth, Latin America and the Caribbean are forecasted to see a 5.2% expansion, while the Asia-Pacific region is expected to grow by 6.9%. These positive growth projections are a result of increased vaccination efforts, fiscal stimulus measures, and improved consumer confidence. However, uncertainties remain, such as the uneven distribution of vaccines and the potential for new variants of the virus. Overall, the global economic outlook for 2021 looks promising, with hopes for a steady recovery in the coming months.
news.headline: S&P 500 gains decline due to tariff concerns as bonds rise. Market snapshot for 5/29/2025.

news.summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and Binance have submitted a collaborative request to resolve a prolonged legal dispute lasting almost two years. This marks a significant retreat by the SEC in its recent crackdown on cryptocurrency-related cases.
news.headline: US regulator SEC withdraws lawsuit against major crypto exchange Binance after nearly 2 years, marking a recent shift in crypto enforcement.
